LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - MS. UCHIYAMA PRESENTED A VALID PROOF SERVICE IN OPEN COURT. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF GARY LINDSEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE ARE TRIER OF FACTS FROM WHICH THE FINDER OF FACTS COULD CONCLUDE THAT THIS WAS BASED ON A SEPARATE UNIT BASED ON THE DECLARATION OF THE DEFENDANT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KAREN Y. UCHIYAMA, TEL. NO. 563-9301 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. JAMES COY DRISCOLL, TEL. NO. 673-6000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - MS. UCHIYAMA PRESENTED A VALID PROOF SERVICE IN OPEN COURT. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF GARY LINDSEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE ARE TRIER OF FACTS FROM WHICH THE FINDER OF FACTS COULD CONCLUDE THAT THIS WAS BASED ON A SEPARATE UNIT BASED ON THE DECLARATION OF THE DEFENDANT. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF GARY LINDSEY'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Admission And Form Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions is granted except as to Form Interrogatory 15.1.
